choices: Tools and knowledge that solve problems
mcq.explain: Technology is any tool or knowledge humans use to solve problems — from a wheel to a smartphone.

mcq.q: Which of these is NOT technology?
mcq.explain: A rainbow is natural — not made by people.

mcq.q: The first wheel was a form of…
mcq.explain: Simple tools like the wheel are among the earliest technology.
Technology saves time, energy, and solves problems.

mcq.q: An example of digital technology is…
mcq.explain: Digital tech uses electronic signals (computers, tablets, phones).

mcq.q: Technology can be used for good and…
choices: Bad — so we must use it wisely
mcq.explain: Like any tool, tech should be used responsibly.

mcq.q: Which is an example of analogue technology?
mcq.explain: Wind-up clocks use mechanical motion — analogue, not digital.

mcq.q: The wheel and axle is one of the…
mcq.explain: It's an ancient simple machine.
Computers use binary digits (bits).

mcq.q: Which of these is assistive technology?
mcq.explain: Assistive tech helps people with disabilities — like hearing aids.

choices: Sources that don't run out (solar, wind)
mcq.explain: Renewables harness sun, wind, water etc.

mcq.q: Smart watches are a form of…
mcq.explain: Wearable tech is worn on the body.
GPS uses satellites to find your location.

choices: Building them up layer by layer
mcq.explain: Additive manufacturing builds layer by layer.
Bluetooth connects devices wirelessly over short distances.
AI imitates human thinking with computers.
Drones fly without a pilot on board.
E-waste is old electronics that need careful recycling.
Better batteries support solar and wind power.
With benefits come risks like privacy and safety.

flashcards.back: Tools and knowledge used to solve problems
Anything that helps you do a task

meta.hook: Build something cool: What is Technology?

lessons.text: Build something cool: What is Technology?

mcq.q: The 'brain' of a computer is the…
mcq.explain: CPU = Central Processing Unit. It does all the thinking.
RAM temporarily holds data the CPU is working on.
Hard drives and SSDs store files permanently.

mcq.q: The physical parts of a computer are called…
mcq.explain: Hardware = things you can touch.

mcq.q: Programs and apps are examples of…
mcq.explain: Software = instructions for the computer.
Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS are OSes.
The CPU is the computer's processor.
A gigabyte is 1 billion bytes (about).
Monitors display output.
You input commands using a mouse.

choices: Programs that tell hardware what to do
mcq.explain: Software is the instructions a computer runs.
Hardware = the tangible parts.

mcq.q: A USB flash drive is for…
mcq.explain: USB flash drives store and carry files.
The OS manages the computer's resources.
Windows is an operating system.

mcq.q: A bit is the smallest unit of data and equals…
mcq.explain: Binary digits are 0 or 1.

choices: On servers accessed via the internet
Inside a cloud in the sky
mcq.explain: Cloud means online servers.
It captures video — input.
BIOS starts the computer.
Firewalls block unwanted network traffic.
HTML = HyperText Markup Language — describes web page structure.

mcq.q: Which tag makes a big heading?
mcq.explain: <h1> is the largest heading; <h6> is smallest.
is the paragraph tag in HTML.
link text creates hyperlinks.
embeds an image.
<!DOCTYPE html> declares the document as HTML5.

mcq.q: Which tag creates a line break?
mcq.explain: is a line break in HTML.

mcq.q: Which tag is for the largest heading?
mcq.explain: <h1> is the largest heading.

mcq.q: To open a link in a new tab, you add the attribute…
mcq.explain: target="_blank" opens in a new tab.
<ul> is an unordered list.
<ol> is an ordered list.

mcq.q: Each item in a list uses…
mcq.explain: <li> is a list item.

mcq.q: Which attribute provides text for an image when it can't be displayed?
mcq.explain: The alt attribute is alternative text for accessibility.

mcq.q: Which tag is for the page's main content area?
mcq.explain: <main> is HTML5 semantic for the main content.

mcq.q: To make text bold semantically use…
mcq.explain: <strong> indicates strong importance and shows bold.

mcq.q: To make text italic semantically use…
mcq.explain: <em> emphasises text in italics.
<head> holds info that doesn't appear on the page.
doesn't need a closing tag.
<a> tags make links.
DOCTYPE specifies the document type.

flashcards.back: Extra info inside a tag (e.g., href)

meta.hook: Build something cool: Introduction to HTML

lessons.text: Build something cool: Introduction to HTML

mcq.explain: CSS = Cascading Style Sheets — styles HTML.
The property 'color' (American spelling) sets text colour.
As named — it sets background.
A dot selects a class. # selects an id.

mcq.q: How do you link an external CSS file?
mcq.explain: <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css"> in the <head>.

mcq.q: Which property controls spacing INSIDE a box?
mcq.explain: Padding is inside; margin is outside.

mcq.q: In CSS, the property that sets text size is…
mcq.explain: font-size controls the size of text.

mcq.q: To make text bold in CSS, use…
mcq.explain: font-weight: bold makes text bold.

mcq.q: #header is a CSS selector that targets…
mcq.explain: # selects by id.
text-align: center centres text.
Margins are outside the box.
Padding sits inside the box, between the content and the border.

mcq.q: To set background colour to red use…
mcq.explain: background-color: red is correct.

mcq.q: In CSS, /* this is a comment */ is the syntax for…
mcq.explain: CSS comments use /* … */.

mcq.q: Which property controls a border thickness?
mcq.explain: border-width sets the line thickness.

mcq.q: Flexbox layout is enabled by display…
mcq.explain: display: flex starts flex layout.
border-radius rounds corners.
Hex codes start with # then 6 hex digits.

mcq.q: To make an image responsive, set width to…
mcq.explain: width:100% scales to its container.
Media queries make designs responsive.

meta.hook: Build something cool: Styling with CSS

lessons.text: Build something cool: Styling with CSS

mcq.explain: JS adds interactivity — buttons, forms, animations.
alert() pops up a message.

mcq.q: Which declares a variable in modern JS?
mcq.explain: var (old), let (changeable), const (unchangeable) — all declare variables.
console.log helps you debug by printing values.
Functions group instructions you can run many times.
addEventListener('click', fn) runs fn when clicked.

mcq.q: To declare a constant in modern JS use…
mcq.explain: const declares a constant.
console.log prints to the console.

mcq.q: To add 2 and 3 in JS, write…
mcq.explain: + adds numbers.

mcq.q: To select an element by id use…
mcq.explain: getElementById selects by id.
if runs the block when the condition evaluates true.

choices: Repeat code a known number of times
mcq.explain: for loops repeat using a counter.
Arrays hold ordered lists.
Use single, double or back-tick quotes.

mcq.q: A function is defined with the keyword…
mcq.explain: function keyword (or arrow function => syntax).
=== is strict; == is loose.

mcq.q: To respond to a button click, you use…
mcq.explain: addEventListener attaches an event handler.
Booleans are true or false.
querySelector returns the first match.
// for one line, /* */ for many.

flashcards.back: User action like click, type, scroll
Runs code when a test is true

mcq.explain: Conductors (metal) let current flow. Insulators (rubber) block it.
Batteries store chemical energy and release electricity.
Switches turn circuits on or off.
Resistors control how much current flows.
LEDs are small, efficient light sources.
Standard symbols make diagrams easy to read.

mcq.q: A complete loop for current to flow is a…
mcq.explain: Closed circuits let current flow.
Breaks prevent current.

mcq.q: A switch in the OFF position creates a(n)…
mcq.explain: OFF means circuit is broken.
Volts measure electric potential.
Amperes (A) measure flow of charge.
Ohms (Ω) measure opposition to current.

mcq.q: A diode allows current to flow…
mcq.explain: Diodes are one-way.
Capacitors store charge for later use.
Series shares the same current.

mcq.q: In parallel circuits, voltage across each branch is…
mcq.explain: Parallel branches have the same voltage.
AC reverses direction many times per second.
DC flows in one direction.

mcq.q: A fuse protects a circuit by…
choices: Breaking when current is too high
mcq.explain: Fuses melt to stop dangerous overloads.

choices: Current bypasses the load through a low-resistance path
mcq.explain: Short circuits can be dangerous.

flashcards.back: Closed path for electricity to flow

mcq.explain: Input devices send data INTO the computer.
Output devices present results TO the user.
It shows output AND receives touch input.
It's an audio input device.
Printers produce physical (printed) output.
Cameras send video/image data to the computer.
Scanners turn paper into digital images — input.
Headphones output sound.

mcq.q: A graphics tablet is mainly an…
mcq.explain: You draw input with the stylus.
It outputs an image to a screen or wall.
They display (output) and respond to touch (input).
They display immersive output and detect head movement.
It reads (inputs) the barcode.
It builds physical objects from data — output.
You input pointer movements with it.
Speakers vibrate to make sound.
Mics turn sound into electrical signals.

mcq.q: A QR code reader on a phone is an…
mcq.explain: It captures the code — input.
Modern screens use LED or LCD.
Plotters move pens to create large prints.

meta.hook: Build something cool: Input & Output Devices

lessons.text: Build something cool: Input & Output Devices

choices: A global network of connected computers
mcq.explain: It links billions of devices across the world.
HTTPS is the secure version of HTTP.
Wi-Fi is wireless radio transmission.

mcq.q: A browser is a program that…
mcq.explain: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge display websites.

mcq.q: Data sent across the Internet travels in small…
mcq.explain: Data is broken into packets and reassembled.
Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo are search engines.
HTTP transfers web data.
HTTPS encrypts data.
URL = Uniform Resource Locator.
DNS lets you type names instead of numbers.

choices: A number that identifies a device on the internet
mcq.explain: Devices have IP addresses to be reachable.

mcq.q: A modem connects you to your…
mcq.explain: Modems link your network to the internet provider.
Routers move packets between networks.

mcq.q: Data on the internet is split into…
mcq.explain: Packets are small data pieces sent separately.
HTTPS and padlock indicate a secure connection.
Spam clutters inboxes.

choices: Trick users into giving sensitive info
mcq.explain: Phishing emails impersonate trusted services.
Length and variety make passwords harder to crack.
2FA makes accounts much safer.

choices: Small data stored by your browser for a site
mcq.explain: Cookies remember preferences and logins.

flashcards.back: World Wide Web — websites on the Internet
Computer that sends info to others

meta.hook: Build something cool: How the Internet Works

lessons.text: Build something cool: How the Internet Works

mcq.q: A word processor is used for…
mcq.explain: MS Word, Google Docs — for text documents.
Excel, Google Sheets handle numbers and formulas.
PowerPoint, Google Slides, Keynote.

choices: Strong passwords and never sharing personal info
mcq.explain: Protect yourself with strong passwords and privacy.
Google Drive, OneDrive, iCloud store files online.

mcq.q: An email address has the symbol…
mcq.explain: user@example.com

mcq.q: A spreadsheet uses cells arranged in…
mcq.explain: Cells form a grid.

choices: The sum of cells A1 through A5
mcq.explain: SUM totals values in a range.

mcq.q: A digital photo's resolution is measured in…
mcq.explain: Pixels are the dots that make up an image.

mcq.q: A PDF is a format good for…
choices: Sharing documents that look the same on any device
mcq.explain: PDFs preserve layout.

choices: Use clear visuals and short text
mcq.explain: Less text + visuals communicates better.
Digital citizenship means responsible online behaviour.

mcq.q: To back up files you can…
choices: Save to cloud or external drive
mcq.explain: Backups protect against data loss.

choices: Trail of data you leave online
mcq.explain: Posts, comments and history form your footprint.

mcq.q: To collaborate online many tools allow…
mcq.explain: Cloud docs allow many editors.

mcq.q: A QR code can be used to…
choices: Quickly open a website on a phone
mcq.explain: Phones scan QR codes to open links.

mcq.q: A virus on a computer is a type of…
mcq.explain: Viruses are malicious software.
Antivirus protects against threats.

choices: Not sharing personal info with strangers
mcq.explain: Keep personal data private online.

choices: A piece of cultural content shared online
mcq.explain: Memes spread fast on social media.

meta.hook: Build something cool: Digital Tools & Apps

lessons.text: Build something cool: Digital Tools & Apps

mcq.explain: Algorithms describe how to solve a problem.

mcq.q: A loop is used when you need to…
mcq.explain: Loops save time by repeating tasks.
if (score > 10) { … } — runs only if score is more than 10.
Fixing bugs is called debugging.

choices: A plan in plain English-like steps
mcq.explain: Pseudocode plans logic before you code.
JS lets you control program logic. HTML is markup, not programming.
Variables hold values.
Functions perform a task and can be reused.

mcq.q: To repeat code while a condition is true, use a…
mcq.explain: While loops keep running until the condition is false.
Booleans store true/false.
Debuggers step through code to inspect it.

mcq.q: An array index usually starts at…
mcq.explain: Most languages use 0-based indexing.

mcq.q: Code that runs only when a button is clicked uses…
mcq.explain: Event listeners react to user actions.
Flowcharts visualise algorithms.
Conditions branch the program.
Comments are ignored by the compiler/interpreter.
Pairs share screen and ideas.

mcq.q: Version control tools like Git track…
mcq.explain: Git records every change to your code.
Syntax errors break the language rules.

mcq.q: A simple 'Hello World' program is often used to…
choices: Test setup of a new language
mcq.explain: Hello World is the classic first program.

flashcards.back: Step-by-step recipe to solve a problem
A test that is true or false
Tool to write code (Python, JS, etc.)

mcq.q: In a calculator app, buttons for digits are usually…
mcq.explain: Clickable items use the <button> element.
+ adds. - subtracts. * multiplies. / divides.

mcq.q: If display = 5 and user clicks '+' then '3' then '=', what should display show?

mcq.explain: parseFloat turns a string into a floating-point number.

mcq.q: To clear the calculator, you might set display to…
mcq.explain: Reset to 0 or empty string.

mcq.q: Why wrap calculator logic in functions?
mcq.explain: Functions keep code clean and reusable.

mcq.q: In a calculator, you can store the running total in a variable like…
mcq.explain: Use let or const to declare the variable.
* is the multiplication operator.
/ is the division operator.
= triggers the calculation.

mcq.q: For decimal input, the dot button must add to the number string only if…
mcq.explain: Avoid two decimal points in one number.
C clears the calculator.

mcq.q: To prevent dividing by zero you can…
mcq.explain: Add validation to avoid errors.

mcq.q: To append a digit to the display string, use…
mcq.explain: String concatenation builds the number text.
Number() converts a string to a number.

mcq.q: To round to 2 decimal places use…
mcq.explain: toFixed(2) returns a string with 2 decimals.

mcq.q: To listen for a button click in JS, use…
mcq.explain: addEventListener is the standard way.

mcq.q: To handle keyboard input you can listen for…
mcq.explain: keydown fires when a key is pressed.

mcq.q: To keep code DRY, repeated logic should be…
mcq.explain: DRY = Don't Repeat Yourself.

mcq.q: To show errors clearly to users you can…
mcq.explain: Helpful messages improve user experience.

flashcards.back: Runs a string as JS (use carefully!)

mcq.explain: localStorage persists across sessions in the browser.
Needed to save arrays/objects in localStorage.
Used when loading data from localStorage.

mcq.q: To add an item to an array 'todos', you use…
mcq.explain: push() appends an item to the end.
# finds an id; . finds classes.

mcq.q: To remove an element from the DOM you can call…
mcq.explain: element.remove() removes it from the page.
localStorage only stores strings — that's why we use JSON.

mcq.q: To save an object to localStorage you should…
mcq.explain: Convert to JSON string before saving.

mcq.q: To load an object from localStorage you should…
mcq.explain: Parse the JSON string back to an object.

mcq.q: To clear all stored data, call…
mcq.explain: clear() removes everything.

mcq.q: To delete a single key, use…
mcq.explain: removeItem removes one key.

mcq.q: A todo list is best stored as…
mcq.explain: Arrays hold ordered collections naturally.

mcq.q: To add to an array, use…
mcq.explain: push appends to the end.

mcq.q: To remove the last array item, use…
mcq.explain: pop removes the last element.

mcq.q: To loop through an array of todos use…
mcq.explain: forEach is concise; for offers more control.

mcq.q: To select all elements with class .todo use…
mcq.explain: querySelectorAll returns all matches.

mcq.q: To create a new HTML element use…
mcq.explain: createElement makes a new node.

mcq.q: To set the text inside an element use…
mcq.explain: textContent sets plain text safely.

mcq.q: To respond to checking off a todo, listen for…
mcq.explain: Checkbox toggles fire change/click events.

mcq.q: Using JSON makes saving complex data…
mcq.explain: JSON converts objects/arrays to a string for storage.

flashcards.back: Add item to end of array
